---
title: Michael Montemurri
subtitle: M.Sc. Mathematics & Statistics, McGill University · 2026 
description: | 
  Montreal, QC, Canada<br>

navbar: true
about:
  id: heading
  template: solana
  image: files/michael.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 185px
---


<!-- Social links bar -->
<div style="
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 1.2rem;
  justify-content: center;
  margin: 1.4rem 0;
  font-size: 1.05rem;
">

  <a href="mailto:mikemontemurri@gmail.com" style="text-decoration: underline;" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    mikemontemurri@gmail.com
  </a>

  <a href="./files/Montemurri_Michael_Resume.pdf" style="text-decoration: underline;" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    CV
  </a>

  <a href="https://github.com/michaelmontemurri" style="text-decoration: underline;" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    GitHub
  </a>

  <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Tj3jzigAAAAJ" style="text-decoration: underline;" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    Google Scholar
  </a>

  <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/michael-montemurri-a0a94b1a9/" style="text-decoration: underline;" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    LinkedIn
  </a>

</div>

## About Me

I recently completed an M.Sc. in Mathematics and Statistics at McGill University and Mila – Quebec AI Institute, where I was supervised by [Prof. Eric Kolaczyk](https://kolaczyk.github.io/).  

My work focuses on **graph representation learning** for molecular property prediction in low-data regimes, combining deep graph models with tree-based methods to improve performance in graph-level prediction tasks.
I am broadly interested in **machine learning on graphs**, including pretraining strategies for graph transformers, foundational graph models, and their applications. 

I hold a B.Sc. in Physics and Applied Mathematics from Bowling Green State University, where I was a member of the NCAA Division I Men’s Soccer Team and a Research Assistant in the [Zamkov Lab](https://physics.bgsu.edu/~zamkovm/), studying quantum dots.

Before graduate school, I worked as a Data Scientist in South Essex Fabricating’s R&D Division.

In Summer 2025, I worked as a Quantitative Strategist Associate at Morgan Stanley on the U.S. Treasuries desk, and will be returning full-time in June 2026.

[View my CV (PDF)](./files/Montemurri_Michael_Resume.pdf)

---
